channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump assassination attempt suspect legal team battles DOJ over evidence, test-firing gun
item.description: Ryan Routh's defense team is demanding more information on the evidence against the alleged would-be Trump assassin. However, the prosecution insists that it has provided information on a regular basis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Democrats vent, cry and issue call to action to counter Musk and Trump
item.description: SAUK CITY, Wis. (AP) — They came to vent and to cry and to exhort. The overflow crowd of frustrated Democrats who met this week in a small Wisconsin city's library voiced anger over President Donald Trump, his billionaire adviser Elon Musk and the direction of the country.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hispanic Caucus demands apology from GOP campaign arm after ‘illegal immigrant’ smear
item.description: The Congressional Hispanic Caucus (CHC) is demanding an apology from the House Republicans’ campaign arm after it falsely portrayed the CHC’s chair as an “illegal immigrant.” The National Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) deleted the controversial social media post Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hospitals warned they must protect children from chemical and surgical mutilation: HHS agency memo
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services sent a memo reminding hospitals and medical providers of new policies restricting gender-transition treatments and surgeries for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is set to speak to crypto founders and investors at the White House on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed an order late Thursday creating a cryptocurrency strategic reserve using bitcoin seized through court proceedings, directing officials to find “budget-neutral” ways to grow it.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota’s budget situation worsens in the near and long term with possible deficit growing
item.description: Minnesota finance officials reported worsening in the state’s budget picture in the near term and down the road, saying a small fiscal cushion had shrunk and a future potential deficit has grown.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump prepares order dismantling the Education Department
item.description: The draft executive action, obtained by NPR, acknowledges the department and its signature responsibilities were created by Congress, and cannot legally be altered without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: A second federal judge has ruled to block the Trump administration’s spending freeze
item.description: U.S. District Judge John McConnell Jr. said the attempt to pause trillions in federal spending “fundamentally undermines the distinct constitutional roles of each branch of our government.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
